## Petaluridae

[â† Back to Petaluridae in Family Index](/field-notes/insects/odonata/anistoptera/family#petaluridae)
{: style="text-align: center;"}

**Petaluridae** (petaltails) are a small, ancient family of very large dragonflies often associated with bogs, fens, and other specialized wetlands. They are less commonly encountered but notable when present.

### Field marks
- Very large body size
- Habitat specialists (bogs, fens, seepage areas)
- Slower, powerful flight compared to many skimmers

### Habitat
Restricted to specialized wetland habitats such as bogs, fen margins, and seepage zones; often locally uncommon.

### Representative species
- Petaltails (family *Petaluridae*)

---
